channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Islamabad reopens transport access and updates business closing hours across the city
item.description: The Islamabad administration has allowed all types of public and goods transport to enter the city, easing earlier restrictions and improving mobility for residents and businesses. However, Faizabad b...

item.title: Opposition Parties Agree to Present One Presidential Candidate for 2027 Election
item.description: On Saturday for Ibadan, leaders from major opposition parties don gather for summit wey Governor Seyi Makinde host. Di summit bring leaders from PDP, ADC, NNPP, plus other political groups together to...

item.title: 32-year-old social media influencer dies following road accident in Soho
item.description: A 32-year-old social media influencer tragically lost their life after being involved in a traffic accident in Soho. The incident, which occurred late in the evening, saw the individual struck by a ve...

item.title: The Rolling Stones tease upcoming album 'Foreign Tongues' with international billboards
item.description: The Rolling Stones are giving fans a glimpse of their next musical endeavour, reportedly titled 'Foreign Tongues', through a series of global billboards displaying the phrase in multiple languages. Th...

item.title: Effective Resistance Exercises to Strengthen Hips and Improve Mobility
item.description: Hips play a central role in almost every movement we perform, from climbing stairs and running after children to standing up from a chair. Building durable hips goes beyond mere flexibility; it requir...

item.title: US and Iran remain at odds as indirect talks planned in Islamabad amid ongoing conflict
item.description: US negotiators are set to travel to Islamabad for discussions aimed at easing tensions with Iran, but Tehran has made it clear that it will not engage in direct talks with American officials. Instead,...

item.title: Lions secure dominant win over Connacht to book historic URC quarter-final spot
item.description: The Lions delivered a commanding performance at Ellis Park, beating Connacht 33-14 to secure their first-ever place in the United Rugby Championship (URC) quarter-finals. The Johannesburg side extende...

item.title: Souths Break 28-Year Hoodoo in Melbourne with a Massive Win Over Storm
item.description: In a historic Anzac Day clash, Melbourne Storm suffered a humiliating 48-6 loss to South Sydney Rabbitohs, marking their sixth consecutive defeat of the season. The Rabbitohs, celebrating their first-...

item.title: Innu Child Welfare Inquiry Commissioners Express Hope for Future After Testimony
item.description: The inquiry into the treatment of Innu families within the child protection system in Newfoundland and Labrador has concluded a week of emotionally challenging testimony. Commissioners Anastasia Qupee...

item.title: Gunfire at multiple Sydney homes linked to gang tensions raises retaliation concerns
item.description: Police in Sydney are investigating a series of shootings targeting three separate homes, believed to be linked to individuals associated with the Alameddine crime network. The incidents have raised se...

item.title: Merebank murder suspect escapes police custody at Wentworth Hospital
item.description: A 54-year-old woman suspected of murdering 82-year-old Indrawathy Rampersadh in Merebank and committing a separate robbery has escaped from police custody at Wentworth Hospital. The suspect, initially...

item.title: ACT Brumbies face back-row turnover as key players head overseas
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are facing a significant turnover in their back-row as Tuaina Taii Tualima nears a move to France, following the recent departure of Luke Reimer. Despite attempts to keep Tualima, coa...

item.title: Ruchir Sharma explains that West Bengal election results do not reflect economic growth trends
item.description: The recent statement by Ruchir Sharma highlights that economic growth in India is increasingly becoming independent of electoral outcomes, specifically in the context of the West Bengal assembly elect...

item.title: Pakistan's Role in Maintaining the Iran-US Truce
item.description: Recent reports indicate that Pakistan is playing a crucial role in the ongoing ceasefire between Iran and the United States. The fragile truce, vital for regional stability, faces potential collapse, ...

item.title: Australian families leave Syrian camp in renewed bid to return home
item.description: A group of Australian women and children have departed Roj camp in north-east Syria in a renewed effort to return to Australia, according to officials and on-ground reporting. The group, made up of fo...

item.title: Rob Reiner's Son Charged in Parents' Murders: Details Emerge
item.description: Rob Reiner, the acclaimed film director, is grappling with a deeply distressing family tragedy. His son, Jake Reiner, has been charged with the murders of Rob's parents, Lynn Loring and Michael Reiner...

item.title: Scarlett Moffatt criticises campmates for overshadowing Adam Thomas' victory moment
item.description: Scarlett Moffatt, a former winner of 'I'm A Celebrity', emotionally defended Adam Thomas on the ITV after-show 'Unpacked'. Moffatt expressed her frustration over the way campmates' behaviour during th...

item.title: SFI Demands Action Against Faculty for Harassing Postgraduate Medical Students in Kerala
item.description: The Students' Federation of India (SFI), the student wing of the CPI(M), has demanded strict action against faculty members at a medical college in Kerala accused of harassing postgraduate (PG) medica...

item.title: Raghav Chadha and the Political Shifts in AAP: An Overview of Defections and Political Instability
item.description: The article highlights the turmoil within the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) in India, focusing on key figures like Raghav Chadha. The party has faced internal rebellions, with prominent leaders exiting over t...

item.title: U.S. expands anti-drug maritime strikes in Latin America amid legal concerns
item.description: A recent U.S. military strike in the eastern Pacific Ocean has killed two individuals aboard a vessel suspected of transporting illegal drugs, according to U.S. Southern Command. The incident is part ...
